enrichment.short_description: Open-source e-signature platform for creating, sending, and signing documents, with cloud or self-hosted deployment options.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Documenso is an open-source e-signature platform with free, team, and self-hosted plans for signing and managing documents.

features.title: Document workflows and templates
features.description: Create reusable, directly-linkable templates to speed up repeated document signing workflows.

features.title: API and webhooks

features.title: Team collaboration
features.description: The Teams plan includes 5 users (expandable) and a shared team workspace for collaborative document signing.

features.title: Embedded and white-label signing

pros: Open-source codebase available on GitHub for transparency and self-hosting
Free plan available with no credit card required (5 documents/month)
Self-hosted licensing option for full infrastructure control
API access included even on the Individual plan for personal automation
States compliance with ESIGN Act, UETA, 21 CFR Part 11, SOC2, and HIPAA

faqs.question: Is Documenso open source?
faqs.answer: Yes, Documenso states its code is open source and available on GitHub, and it can be self-hosted via Docker.

faqs.question: Does Documenso have a free plan?
faqs.answer: Yes, the Free plan allows up to 5 documents per month with up to 10 recipients per document, and no credit card is required.

faqs.question: Can I self-host Documenso?
faqs.answer: Yes, Documenso offers a self-hosted licensing option in addition to its hosted cloud service.

faqs.question: What compliance standards does Documenso support?
faqs.answer: Documenso states support for the ESIGN Act, UETA, 21 CFR Part 11, SOC2, and HIPAA.

faqs.question: Does Documenso offer an API?
faqs.answer: Yes, API access is included starting with the Individual plan, with unlimited API access on the Platform plan.

use_cases.title: Individual document signing
use_cases.description: Individuals who need to sign or send a low volume of documents can use the free plan or the Individual plan for unlimited documents.

use_cases.title: Team contract workflows
use_cases.description: The Teams plan supports a shared workspace and embedded signing for collaborative document workflows.

use_cases.title: Custom product integration
use_cases.description: The Platform plan offers unlimited API access and white-label embedded signing for companies building signing into their own products.

use_cases.title: Self-hosted deployment
use_cases.description: Organizations that require full infrastructure control can self-host Documenso instead of using the hosted cloud version.

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Documenso?
paragraphs: Documenso is an electronic signature platform for creating, sending, and signing documents online, with tools for building signing workflows and reusable templates.
The product is open source, with its code published on GitHub, and can be used as a hosted cloud service or self-hosted on your own infrastructure via Docker.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Documenso is for
paragraphs: Documenso serves individuals who need a free or low-cost way to sign occasional documents, as well as teams and platform builders who need API access, embedded signing, and white-label options.

enrichment.short_description: Open-source e-signature and document workflow platform for legally binding contracts, offering free, cloud, and self-hosted plans.

features.title: Self-hosted and cloud deployment

faqs.question: Is DocuSeal open source?
faqs.answer: Yes, DocuSeal offers a free, open-source on-premises plan supported through GitHub, alongside its hosted cloud plans.

faqs.question: Does DocuSeal have a free plan?
faqs.answer: Yes, the Basic cloud plan is free and includes legally-binding e-signatures, indefinite document retention, and up to 10 signature request emails per month.

faqs.question: Can DocuSeal be self-hosted?
faqs.answer: Yes, DocuSeal provides an Open Source on-premises plan as well as a paid on-premises Pro license for full data control.

faqs.question: Does DocuSeal charge extra for identity verification?
faqs.answer: Yes, ID verification costs $2 per verification and Qualified Electronic Signature costs $2-4 per signature.

faqs.question: Does DocuSeal support AI features?
faqs.answer: DocuSeal's site highlights new AI-native workflow capabilities.

use_cases.title: Individual e-signing
use_cases.description: Individuals can use the free Basic or Open Source plan for legally-binding signatures with indefinite document retention.

use_cases.title: Business compliance and branding
use_cases.description: The Pro plan adds custom branding, automated reminders, SSO/SAML, and bulk sending for organizations with compliance and workflow needs.
use_cases.audience: Businesses in healthcare, financial services, real estate, and education

use_cases.title: Developer-embedded signing
use_cases.description: The signing API and React component let developers embed document signing directly into their own products.

use_cases.title: Data-sensitive self-hosting
use_cases.description: The on-premises Open Source and Pro license plans let organizations run DocuSeal on their own infrastructure for full data control.

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Docuseal?
paragraphs: DocuSeal is an open-source document signing platform positioned as an alternative to DocuSign and PandaDoc, supporting legally-binding e-signatures.
It can be deployed as a cloud-hosted service, including a GDPR-compliant EU Cloud option, or self-hosted on-premises, with an open-source edition supported via GitHub.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Docuseal is for
paragraphs: DocuSeal is used by individuals seeking a free e-signature tool as well as businesses in software, healthcare, real estate, education, and financial services that need compliance, branding, and API-driven signing workflows.

paragraphs: SignRequest is an electronic signature platform that lets users sign contracts and other documents without printing, signing by hand, and scanning.
It offers a free plan for occasional use as well as paid Professional and Business plans with templates, bulk send, and integrations such as Salesforce, Slack, and Zapier.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who SignRequest is for

Organizations needing API access, Salesforce integration, or Single Sign-On can use the Business plan.

faqs.question: When was Scrive founded?
faqs.answer: Scrive was founded in 2010 in Stockholm, Sweden by two university friends.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Scrive is for

84% customer satisfaction, versus a 75% industry average cited on the site
Average support response time of about 10 minutes
Onboarding completed in under 5 business days
Broad support for national eIDs across multiple European countries
cons: No free plan — only a free trial is offered

faqs.question: Does Penneo offer a free trial?
faqs.answer: Yes, Penneo offers a free trial upon request; there is no free plan.

faqs.question: What signature types does Penneo support?
faqs.answer: Penneo supports both Qualified Electronic Signatures (QES) and Advanced Electronic Signatures (AdES) under the EU eIDAS regulation.

faqs.question: Which national eIDs work with Penneo?
faqs.answer: Penneo supports MitID/MitID Erhverv, BankID, itsme/.beID, AusweisApp, and passport verification via ID Verifier.

faqs.question: How many signatures are included in a plan?
faqs.answer: All plans include 500 signatures annually, with additional signatures billed at a per-signature rate and volume discounts available.

faqs.question: What is Penneo's cancellation policy?
faqs.answer: Cancellation requires 3 months' notice, and standard payment terms are 14 days.

use_cases.title: Signing audited financial documents
use_cases.description: Accounting and audit firms use Penneo to collect legally binding signatures on annual reports and audit documentation.

use_cases.title: Financial and banking agreements
use_cases.description: Finance and banking organizations use Penneo's eID-based signing for regulated document workflows.

use_cases.title: Legal document execution
use_cases.description: Legal service providers use Penneo to execute contracts and legal documents with qualified electronic signatures.

use_cases.title: Real estate transactions
use_cases.description: Real estate businesses use Penneo to manage signing for property-related documentation.

use_cases.title: HR and administrative paperwork
use_cases.description: HR and administration teams use Penneo to handle employee-related document signing.

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Penneo?
paragraphs: Penneo is a certified digital trust platform based in Copenhagen, Denmark, that lets businesses send, sign, and manage documents digitally with legally binding signatures.

It supports nationally recognized eIDs such as MitID (Denmark), BankID (Norway/Sweden), itsme/.beID (Belgium), and AusweisApp (Germany), and complies with the EU eIDAS regulation for both Qualified and Advanced Electronic Signatures.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Penneo is for

long_form_blocks.heading: What is GatherUp?
paragraphs: GatherUp is a reputation management platform that helps businesses and agencies collect, monitor, and respond to online reviews. It combines review generation via email and SMS, centralized monitoring across more than 100 review sites, and AI-generated reply suggestions in one dashboard.

The platform also includes fake review detection and dispute filing tools, listings syndication through its Listings Hub add-on, and a white-label Agency plan that lets marketing agencies resell reputation management services under their own brand.

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Marqii?

It is used by more than 15,000 restaurants, including brands like Tacodeli, Insomnia Cookies, Deschutes Brewery, and Cooper's Hawk, to keep listings and menus consistent across 70+ platforms.
